executive_brief: The Expense Tracker is a lightweight, client-side personal finance application designed for single-user tracking. It enables users to record expenses with specific attributes (amount, date, category, description) and monitor spending via a summary dashboard. The system follows a stateless architecture leveraging browser LocalStorage for data persistence, explicitly omitting user authentication and backend integration for the v1 release.

architectural_constraints: No user authentication or multi-tenant support
Strict v1 scope: No edit/update functionality (Delete and re-add only)
Data limit: Default display of 50 most recent entries with 'Load more' pagination

maturity_assessment: The project specifications are logically comprehensive and well-defined, but the current state is BLOCKED. While functional requirements are fully detailed, there are 8 unresolved uncertainties regarding edge-case handling (e.g., storage overflows and large numerical limits) that must be settled to ensure robust implementation and prevent runtime crashes in the browser environment.
critical_dependencies: Modern browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge latest 2 versions)
